project: Influx v1 — PRD 06: Repair Sweep + influx:text-terminal Classifier + Retry-Order Advancement
description: Implement the per-profile repair sweep that drives durable retry for failed enrichment, archive download, and abstract-only re-extraction entirely through Lithos-owned tags (master PRD §6.18, §9.7 repair clause). At the start of every scheduled run and every manual `POST /runs` for a profile, Influx calls `lithos_list(tags=['influx:repair-needed', f'profile:{profile}'], limit=repair.max_items_per_run, order_by='updated_at', order='asc')` and visits up to N notes. For each visited note, the sweep independently selects retry stages (archive download retry, text-extraction retry, abstract-only re-extraction, Tier 2 retry, Tier 3 retry) by inspecting the current Influx-owned tag set. The new tag `influx:text-terminal` is introduced and owned by this PRD: it marks `text:abstract-only` notes whose archive has been successfully re-extracted and still yielded no better text, and it carries a high-score terminal exemption that lets such notes clear `influx:repair-needed` even without `full-text` / `influx:deep-extracted`. The central invariant — retry-order advancement — requires that every visited note (succeeded, partially succeeded, or failed) is rewritten via `lithos_write` so Lithos bumps `updated_at` and the cursor moves on. The sole exemption is chronic `content_too_large` on the repair path, which leaves `updated_at` unchanged but does NOT abort the run. Backfills (`POST /backfills`) explicitly skip the sweep (FR-REP-2). The sweep runs inside the same per-profile serialised coordinator slot as the rest of that profile's run (FR-REP-4) — never opens a second concurrent slot. PRD 07's extraction workers and Tier 2 / Tier 3 enrichment workers are imported as test-injectable callables (`re_extract_archive`, `tier2_enrich`, `tier3_extract`); this PRD ships stable hook signatures and integration tests that drive each of the three abstract-only outcomes (Upgrade / Terminal / Transient failure).

userStories.title: Expose lithos_read and lithos_list ordering on lithos_client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/lithos_client.py` to expose `lithos_read` and a `lithos_list` that accepts `order_by` and `order` parameters so that the repair sweep can fetch the oldest-by-`updated_at` slice of `influx:repair-needed` + `profile:{profile}` notes (PRD 06 §6 Modify, §5.1 FR-REP-1).
acceptanceCriteria: The Lithos client abstraction in `src/influx/lithos_client.py` exposes a list operation (whether as a `LithosClient` method, a module-level wrapper, or equivalent) that accepts `order_by` and `order` parameters and forwards them to the underlying `lithos_list` MCP call (FR-REP-1)

The Lithos client abstraction exposes a note-read operation (whether as a `LithosClient` method or equivalent wrapper) that returns the current note state for a given identifier, available to the sweep's per-note re-read step in §5.2
A unit test asserts that invoking the list operation with `order_by='updated_at'` and `order='asc'` forwards those parameters to the underlying MCP `list` call
userStories.notes: Implementation guidance: if the in-repo fake Lithos test double used by `lithos_client` tests does not already model `order_by`/`order`, extend it as part of this story so the forwarding test is satisfiable. This is test-harness maintenance, not an acceptance criterion, and `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` is NOT in scope for this PRD.

userStories.title: Add repair.max_items_per_run config knob (default 100, dedicated)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a dedicated `repair.max_items_per_run` config knob (default 100) so the sweep batch size can be tuned without coupling to feedback config (PRD 06 §5.1 FR-REP-1).
acceptanceCriteria: `repair.max_items_per_run` is added to the config schema with default value 100
A config that sets `repair.max_items_per_run = 50` validates successfully and the value is accessible from the runtime config object
The knob MUST NOT be aliased to `feedback.negative_examples_per_profile` — a unit test asserts the two values are independently configurable and can differ

userStories.title: Define repair worker hook protocol (re_extract_archive, tier2_enrich, tier3_extract)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stable, test-injectable hook signatures for `re_extract_archive`, `tier2_enrich`, and `tier3_extract` so that this PRD's sweep can call PRD 07's workers as opaque callables without importing PRD 07's implementation (PRD 06 §4 Internal seams, §3 Out of scope).
acceptanceCriteria: Stable, documented, test-injectable hook callables are declared in a module the sweep imports: `re_extract_archive(note, archive_path)` (signature explicitly required by PRD §4 / §5.2), plus `tier2_enrich` and `tier3_extract` as opaque callables PRD 07 will fill in without changing the interface

The `re_extract_archive` hook's return value discriminates the three abstract-only outcomes — Upgrade (yields `text:html` or `text:pdf`), Terminal (successful extraction yielded abstract-quality text), Transient failure (extraction failed after retry budget) — per §5.2 abstract-only bullet
Each hook may raise `ExtractionError` or `LithosError` and the sweep treats raises as the corresponding 'failed this stage' outcome
Hook implementations can be substituted by tests (e.g. via dependency injection, fixture override, or equivalent), without mandating any specific registry pattern

userStories.title: Create repair.py sweep entry point with lithos_list call
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/repair.py` with a `sweep(profile)` entry point that calls `lithos_list(tags=['influx:repair-needed', f'profile:{profile}'], limit=repair.max_items_per_run, order_by='updated_at', order='asc')` and iterates the results so that subsequent stories can hang stage selection, clearing, and rewrite logic off this loop (PRD 06 §5.1 FR-REP-1, §6 Create).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/repair.py` is created with a `sweep(profile: str)` function (or equivalent) that fetches up to `repair.max_items_per_run` notes via `lithos_list` with the exact tag set, limit, and ordering specified by FR-REP-1
A unit test asserts `lithos_list` is called with `tags=['influx:repair-needed', f'profile:{profile}']`, the configured `limit`, `order_by='updated_at'`, and `order='asc'`
The sweep iterates returned notes in the order Lithos returned them (oldest `updated_at` first) and re-reads each via `lithos_read` before further processing (§5.2)
When `lithos_list` returns zero candidates the sweep returns cleanly without raising and without calling `lithos_read`

userStories.title: Implement per-note stage selection logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want per-note stage selection in `repair.py` that independently picks Archive retry, Text-extraction retry, Abstract-only re-extraction, Tier 2 retry, and Tier 3 retry based on the current Influx-owned tag set so that any subset of outstanding stages is exercised in one pass (PRD 06 §5.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Archive retry is selected iff `influx:archive-missing` is present, regardless of any `text:*` tag (closes FR-ST-4 / AC-06-A)
Text-extraction retry is selected iff no `text:*` tag is present; if both archive and text-extraction stages are selected this pass, the archive stage runs first

Abstract-only re-extraction is selected iff `text:abstract-only` is present AND `influx:text-terminal` is absent AND (the archive stage succeeded this pass OR the note already stores a non-empty `path:` line in `## Archive`)
Tier 2 retry is selected iff `full-text` is missing AND current max profile score ≥ `thresholds.full_text` AND `influx:text-terminal` is absent
Tier 3 retry is selected iff `influx:deep-extracted` is missing AND current max profile score ≥ `thresholds.deep_extract` AND `influx:text-terminal` is absent

userStories.title: Unit tests for stage selection across tag-combination matrix
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering the stage-selection logic over a matrix of tag combinations so the bug class behind FR-ST-4 cannot regress (PRD 06 §9 tests required, AC-06-A).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/unit/test_repair_stage_selection.py` is created and covers at minimum: archive-missing only; text:abstract-only only; archive-missing + text:abstract-only; high-score deep-extract eligible; influx:text-terminal present
AC-06-A regression test: a note tagged `text:html` AND `influx:archive-missing` selects the archive stage but NOT the text-extraction stage
Test asserting Tier 2 / Tier 3 are NOT selected when `influx:text-terminal` is present even with score ≥ thresholds
Test asserting abstract-only re-extraction is NOT selected when `influx:text-terminal` is present
Test asserting abstract-only re-extraction IS selected when archive path is already stored even if no archive stage runs this pass
All new tests pass via `pytest`

userStories.title: Implement tag-clearing rules for archive-missing and repair-needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the post-stage tag-clearing logic in `repair.py` to remove `influx:archive-missing` and `influx:repair-needed` exactly per §5.3, including the high-score terminal exemption (AC-X-7), so partial repairs leave the right tags in place and complete repairs clear cleanly (PRD 06 §5.3, AC-X-7 high-score clearing, AC-06-B, AC-06-C).
acceptanceCriteria: `influx:archive-missing` is removed iff a non-empty `path:` line is stored in `## Archive` on this pass (FR-NOTE-9)

`influx:repair-needed` is removed iff (a) a non-empty `## Archive` `path:` line is stored, AND (b) the note carries `text:html` or `text:pdf` OR (`text:abstract-only` accompanied by `influx:text-terminal`), AND (c) Tier 2 satisfied when score ≥ `thresholds.full_text` AND `influx:text-terminal` absent, AND (d) Tier 3 satisfied when score ≥ `thresholds.deep_extract` AND `influx:text-terminal` absent
High-score terminal exemption (AC-X-7): a note with `influx:text-terminal` is treated as a complete terminal state — Tier 2 and Tier 3 are NOT required for clearing `influx:repair-needed`
AC-06-C: a `text:abstract-only` note WITHOUT `influx:text-terminal` is NEVER cleared from `influx:repair-needed`, even if archive + Tier 2 + Tier 3 all succeed in this pass
Partially-completed repair keeps `influx:repair-needed` and every unsatisfied stage-specific tag in place

userStories.title: Unit tests for tag-clearing rules including high-score terminal exemption
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests that exercise the clearing rules over the same tag-combination matrix as stage selection so the high-score-terminal exemption and the abstract-only-without-terminal lock are both nailed down (PRD 06 §9, AC-X-7, AC-06-B, AC-06-C).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/unit/test_repair_clearing_rules.py` is created and covers: archive-missing only; text:abstract-only only; archive-missing + text:abstract-only; high-score deep-extract eligible; `influx:text-terminal` present
AC-06-B test: archive download + abstract-only Upgrade in one pass clears BOTH `influx:archive-missing` and `influx:repair-needed` (assuming no other outstanding-stage tag remains)
AC-06-C negative test: a `text:abstract-only` note WITHOUT `influx:text-terminal` is NOT cleared even when archive + Tier 2 + Tier 3 all succeeded this pass

AC-X-7 high-score-terminal positive test: a terminal-abstract-only note with score ≥ `thresholds.deep_extract`, archive path stored, and no other outstanding-stage tag is cleared from `influx:repair-needed`
All new tests pass via `pytest`

userStories.title: Implement influx:text-terminal three-outcome semantics for abstract-only re-extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the abstract-only re-extraction stage to discriminate Upgrade / Terminal / Transient outcomes from `re_extract_archive` and apply tag changes per §5.2 so the new `influx:text-terminal` tag is set exactly when the extractor succeeded but yielded no better text (PRD 06 §5.2 abstract-only bullet, §5.8 FR-TAG-1 delta, AC-X-7).
acceptanceCriteria: Upgrade outcome: replace `text:abstract-only` with the upgraded `text:html` or `text:pdf` tag; `influx:text-terminal` is NOT added
Terminal outcome: keep `text:abstract-only`; ADD `influx:text-terminal`
Transient failure outcome: keep `text:abstract-only` and `influx:repair-needed`; do NOT add `influx:text-terminal` — the note re-enters the sweep on a later run
`influx:text-terminal` is NEVER set on the initial write of a note (per AC-M2-3) — only as a result of this stage's Terminal outcome
Exhausting the extractor's retry budget within a single pass (Transient failure) is NOT terminal
Hook integration: this stage calls the `re_extract_archive` callable from US-003 and switches on its discriminator

userStories.title: Unit tests for influx:text-terminal three-outcome semantics
userStories.description: As a developer, I want focused unit tests for `influx:text-terminal` semantics covering all three outcomes so the tag's lifecycle is locked down (PRD 06 §9, AC-X-7).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/unit/test_text_terminal.py` is created
Upgrade-outcome test: fake `re_extract_archive` returns Upgrade; resulting tag set replaces `text:abstract-only` with `text:html` (or `text:pdf`) and does NOT contain `influx:text-terminal`
Terminal-outcome test: fake returns Terminal; resulting tag set keeps `text:abstract-only` and ADDS `influx:text-terminal`
Transient-failure test: fake returns Transient (or raises `ExtractionError`); tag set keeps `text:abstract-only` AND `influx:repair-needed`, and does NOT add `influx:text-terminal`
Initial-write negative test: a note that has never been through abstract-only re-extraction does NOT carry `influx:text-terminal` (AC-M2-3)
All new tests pass via `pytest`

userStories.title: Implement rewrite-on-every-visit retry-order advancement invariant
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every note visited by the sweep — succeeded, partially succeeded, or failed — to be rewritten via `lithos_write` before moving to the next candidate so Lithos bumps `updated_at` and the cursor advances, providing the fairness guarantee (PRD 06 §5.4, AC-X-8).
acceptanceCriteria: Every visited note goes through the `lithos_write` path (per the rules in §9.5 of `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`) before the sweep advances to the next candidate — on a `version_conflict` from `lithos_write`, the sweep MUST perform one re-read + re-merge + retry per FR-MCP-7 within that path before treating the conflict as unresolved (AC-06-F first half)
A repair that made no forward progress STILL re-emits the Influx-owned tag set so the write path runs and `updated_at` is bumped

An unresolved `version_conflict` after the FR-MCP-7 re-read + re-merge + retry aborts the run per FR-RES-3, `updated_at` does NOT advance, AND readiness becomes degraded per §5.4 failure mode 1 (AC-06-F second half)
A generic write transport failure that remains unresolved after the existing retry budget aborts the run per FR-RES-3
After a terminal write failure no later candidate is rewritten in that run, and the next run revisits the same head note

userStories.title: Implement chronic content_too_large exemption on the repair path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want chronic `content_too_large` on the repair path to be the sole exemption from retry-order advancement: the sweep continues, the note's `updated_at` does NOT advance, the run does NOT abort, and the note stays at the head of the `updated_at asc` list (PRD 06 §5.4 failure mode 2, AC-X-8 chronic exemption).
acceptanceCriteria: When PRD 05's `content_too_large` wrapper returns `content_too_large_skipped` on the repair path, the existing stored note remains untouched (its persisted body and tags are unchanged from before the visit), the event is logged + counted in `content_too_large_skipped`, and the sweep proceeds to the next candidate — underlying write attempts inside the wrapper are permitted
The run does NOT abort on this exemption; the sweep continues to the next candidate
The note's `updated_at` does NOT advance (since no write succeeded), so it remains at the head of the `updated_at asc` list until either a future trimmed write succeeds or an operator intervenes
Other repair-needed notes in the same sweep still make forward progress (their writes happen, their `updated_at` advances)
The fairness guarantee in §5.5 explicitly excludes notes in this chronic-oversize state (behavioral exclusion)

userStories.title: Wire repair.sweep into service.py for scheduled and manual POST /runs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/service.py` to call `repair.sweep(profile)` at the start of every scheduled run and every manual `POST /runs` for that profile, before the normal source fetch + filter pass, so the sweep runs on every non-backfill profile run (PRD 06 §6 Modify, §5.1 FR-REP-1).
acceptanceCriteria: The scheduled run path (wherever it is hosted in the codebase) invokes `repair.sweep(profile)` at the start of every scheduled run for that profile, before source fetch + filter
The manual `POST /runs` request path (wherever it is hosted in the codebase) invokes `repair.sweep(profile)` at the start of every manual run for that profile, before source fetch + filter

Fatal sweep/write failures (failure mode 1 in §5.4 — generic write transport failure or unresolved `version_conflict` after the FR-MCP-7 re-read + re-merge + retry, after the existing retry budget is exhausted) abort the current repair/run attempt and flow through the existing scheduled and manual run wrappers per §5.4; transient per-stage failures (e.g. abstract-only Transient outcome from `re_extract_archive`) and chronic `content_too_large` on the repair path (failure mode 2 in §5.4) do NOT abort — the sweep continues to the next candidate per §5.2 / §5.4
userStories.notes: The sweep-before-fetch ordering for both scheduled and manual paths is exercised by the broader integration tests in US-016..US-020; a dedicated ordering-only test is optional.

userStories.title: Backfill exclusion: POST /backfills MUST NOT call the sweep
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `POST /backfills` to skip the repair sweep entirely so backfills do not interfere with normal-run retry ordering (PRD 06 §5.7 FR-REP-2, AC-06-D).
acceptanceCriteria: Sweep entry point checks `kind != 'backfill'` (or equivalent) and is NOT invoked from the backfill code path
Positive test: a scheduled run for a profile invokes the sweep exactly once
Negative test (AC-06-D): a `POST /backfills` request for the same profile does NOT invoke the sweep — asserted by spying on `repair.sweep` and confirming zero calls during the backfill request

userStories.title: Coordinator slot integration: sweep runs inside per-profile slot, no second slot
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the sweep to run inside the same per-profile serialised coordinator slot as the rest of that profile's run, never opening a second concurrent slot (PRD 06 §5.6 FR-REP-4, AC-06-E).
acceptanceCriteria: The sweep runs inside the same per-profile coordinator slot already held by the scheduled or manual run path that invoked it; it does NOT acquire a second slot, regardless of which module hosts the call site
AC-06-E test: two profiles' sweeps each run inside their respective per-profile coordinator slots; the lock is held for the union of (sweep + normal fetch), not held twice for the same profile run
Test verifies that across two profiles A and B, sweep+fetch for A runs serialised within A's slot while B's run can proceed independently within B's slot

userStories.title: Integration test: archive-only repair flow (AC-X-4)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test covering the full AC-X-4 oversize-abort + repair flow so the archive stage's independent retry is verified end-to-end (PRD 06 §6 Create, AC-X-4).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/integration/test_repair_sweep_archive_only.py` is created
Test seeds a note tagged `influx:repair-needed` + `influx:archive-missing` with empty `## Archive`, and asserts the EXACT initial body of `## Archive` (per AC-X-4)

Next run's sweep retries the archive download independently of the `text:*` tag, writes the `path:` line on success, removes `influx:archive-missing`, and removes `influx:repair-needed` if no other outstanding-stage tag remains
Test asserts the EXACT post-repair body of `## Archive` (non-empty `path:` line) per AC-X-4
Test passes via `pytest`
userStories.notes: Explicit `text:*`-present coverage for the FR-ST-4 / AC-06-A archive-vs-text-extraction stage-selection scenario lives in the stage-selection unit-test story (US-006); this integration test may include such a variant but is not required to.

userStories.title: Integration test: abstract-only re-extraction three outcomes (AC-X-7)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test exercising all three AC-X-7 outcomes — Upgrade / Terminal / Transient failure — so the abstract-only re-extraction state machine is verified end-to-end with a fake `re_extract_archive` (PRD 06 §6 Create, AC-X-7 (a)/(b)/(c)).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/integration/test_repair_sweep_abstract_only.py` is created

Outcome (a) Upgrade test: fake hook returns Upgrade; final tag set has `text:html` or `text:pdf`, no `influx:text-terminal`, and `influx:repair-needed` is cleared (assuming no other outstanding-stage tag remains)
Outcome (b) Terminal test: fake hook returns Terminal; final tag set keeps `text:abstract-only` AND adds `influx:text-terminal`

Outcome (c) Transient-failure test: fake hook returns Transient (or raises `ExtractionError`); final tag set keeps `text:abstract-only` AND `influx:repair-needed` AND does NOT add `influx:text-terminal`; note remains in the sweep on a subsequent run
Each outcome starts from a note tagged `text:abstract-only` + `influx:repair-needed` with a non-empty `path:` line in `## Archive`
Test passes via `pytest`

userStories.title: Integration test: high-score-terminal clearing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test for the high-score-terminal clearing exemption so a Terminal abstract-only note with score ≥ `thresholds.deep_extract` clears `influx:repair-needed` even without `full-text` / `influx:deep-extracted` (PRD 06 §6 Create, AC-X-7 high-score clearing).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/integration/test_repair_sweep_high_score_terminal.py` is created

Test seeds: max profile score = 9 (≥ `thresholds.deep_extract`), terminal abstract-only note (carries `text:abstract-only` + `influx:text-terminal`), non-empty `path:` line in `## Archive`, no other outstanding-stage tag
After ONE repair pass, the note carries no `influx:repair-needed` even though it has neither `full-text` nor `influx:deep-extracted`
On the NEXT sweep, the same note is NOT re-selected (it no longer matches the `influx:repair-needed` filter)
Test passes via `pytest`

userStories.title: Integration test: retry-order advancement (AC-X-8)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test for the AC-X-8 main case so the rewrite-on-every-visit invariant and the fairness guarantee are verified end-to-end with B > `repair.max_items_per_run` persistently-failing notes (PRD 06 §6 Create, AC-X-8 main).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/integration/test_repair_sweep_advancement.py` is created

Test seeds B persistently-failing notes where B > `repair.max_items_per_run` = M; 'persistently-failing' means repair did not progress but `lithos_write` itself succeeded (chronic-oversize-on-repair does NOT apply)
Two successive runs together visit ≥ `min(B, 2 × M)` distinct notes
No note visited in run K is in the first-N oldest-by-`updated_at` slice of run K+1 unless every other repair-needed note has already been visited in the intervening runs

Asserts the rewrite-on-every-visit invariant: every visited note's `updated_at` advances after its sweep visit, in retry order (test technique is unconstrained — direct `updated_at` observation, write-spy on `lithos_write`, or any equivalent evidence is acceptable)
Test passes via `pytest`

userStories.title: Integration test: chronic content_too_large exemption (AC-X-8 exemption)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test for the chronic-oversize exemption so the sweep is proven to (i) not abort, (ii) not advance the offending note's `updated_at`, (iii) keep that note at the head of the list, and (iv) let other notes still make progress (PRD 06 §6 Create, AC-X-8 chronic exemption).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/integration/test_repair_sweep_chronic_oversize.py` is created
Test seeds one note that hits a second `content_too_large` on the repair path (chronic-oversize) and several other normal repair-needed notes

Across ≥ 2 runs the test asserts (i) the run does NOT abort, (ii) the chronic-oversize note's `updated_at` does NOT advance, (iii) it stays at the head of the `updated_at asc` list, (iv) other repair-needed notes still make progress (their `updated_at` advances and `influx:repair-needed` is removed where applicable)

Asserts the chronic-oversize note on the repair path is both (i) skipped (existing stored note untouched, sweep continues) AND (ii) logged and counted as `content_too_large_skipped` per §5.4 failure mode 2 — observable via any internal counter, in-memory metric, or log assertion (no specific run-stats schema or `notifications.py` surface required)
Test passes via `pytest`

userStories.title: Sweep tag-merge honours influx:rejected:<profile> rejection authority (AC-M3-6)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the sweep's tag-merge step to honour the rejection authority defined in FR-NOTE-6 so a repair pass on a note with `influx:rejected:<profile>` does NOT re-add `profile:<profile>` even if the source matches that profile again (PRD 06 §8 AC-M3-6).
acceptanceCriteria: Sweep's per-note rewrite/tag-merge step preserves any existing `influx:rejected:<profile_B>` tag and does NOT re-add `profile:<profile_B>` for a profile recorded as rejected, even if the source matches that profile again

Unit/helper-level merge test: invoking the sweep's tag-merge helper (e.g. `merge_tags` and/or `build_profile_relevance_for_rewrite`) for a sweep on selected profile `A` against a note carrying `influx:rejected:<profile_B>` plus `profile:A` plus `influx:repair-needed`, where the source would otherwise match both `A` and `B` again, results in: (i) `influx:rejected:<profile_B>` preserved, (ii) `profile:<profile_B>` NOT (re-)added, (iii) the note's `## Profile Relevance` section NOT created or refreshed for `profile_B` during the repair pass (per FR-NOTE-6 rejection authority — the body assertion makes sure the rule is honoured beyond the tag set alone)

Negative test: the same merge step on a note WITHOUT `influx:rejected:<profile_B>` is NOT affected by this rule — `profile:<profile_B>` may be (re-)added when the source matches and the corresponding `## Profile Relevance` entry may be (re-)created/refreshed normally
userStories.notes: Scope clarification: the source-side filter logic itself lives in PRD 09; this PRD's responsibility is only the sweep's merge-time honour of FR-NOTE-6. The acceptance criteria above codify that scope.

userStories.title: Quality gates: ruff + pyright + pytest green; coverage ≥ 80% on repair.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full quality bar from PRD 06 §10 Definition of Done to be green so the PRD can be merged (PRD 06 §10).
acceptanceCriteria: The repo's Ruff lint and format checks pass via the repo's defined lint gate (`make lint`, equivalent to `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` and `uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/`)
The repo's Pyright typecheck passes via the repo's defined typecheck gate (`make typecheck`, equivalent to `uv run pyright`)
`pytest` runs the entire suite green, including all new unit and integration tests added by US-006, US-008, US-010, US-016, US-017, US-018, US-019, US-020, US-021
Coverage on `src/influx/repair.py` is ≥ 80%
Hook signatures `re_extract_archive`, `tier2_enrich`, `tier3_extract` are documented (docstring or module-level comment) and stable so PRD 07 can fill them in without changing the interface
Backfill flow is verified by test to NOT trigger the sweep (US-014)
Coordinator slot is held exactly once per profile run, including the sweep portion (US-015)
